key_insight: A minimizing-movement discretization can decouple normal geometric evolution from tangential mesh motion, while preserving energy stability through admissible constraints that keep the identity map available as a comparator.
engineering_relevance: The framework provides stable parametric finite element schemes for curvature-driven surface evolution, useful for robust simulation of mean curvature flow and surface diffusion with improved mesh quality control.
method: Use a time-stepping variational problem with a normal-dissipation metric plus surface Dirichlet energy.
Choose the metric to encode the flow: L2 for mean curvature flow, H-1 for surface diffusion.
Impose admissible weak tangential constraints so tangential motion can be selected independently of the geometric evolution.
evidence_signal: The paper proves existence, uniqueness, and unconditional energy stability for the fully discrete schemes and reports numerical experiments comparing admissible and classical variants.
watch_item: Practical performance depends on nondegeneracy assumptions and on how the tangential constraints are implemented without degrading mesh quality or solver robustness.
paper_title: A minimizing-movement framework for geometric gradient flows with admissible tangential motion
